title: HMS Dreadnought
content: Several ships and one submarine of the Royal Navy have borne the name HMS "Dreadnought" in the expectation that they would "dread nought", i.e. "fear nothing". The 1906 ship was one of the Royal Navy's most famous vessels; battleships built after her were referred to as' dreadnought s', and earlier battleships became known as pre-dreadnought s. Also Citations and references. Citations References
